songs.name: My Heart Is Buried in Venice
Tears
songs.comments: The song cuts off very suddenly. I definitely think you could justify raising the background score

songs.name: Brain Damage
songs.album: The Dark Side of the Moon

songs.name: Clocks
songs.album: A Rush of Blood to the Head

songs.name: Shoot to Thrill
songs.comments: Lyrics aren't bad but again I wish there was more variance in the second half.
Some of the sections are a little odd. You could argue that the vocal score should be higher.
songs.name: Welcome To The Jungle
jar of hearts
songs.comments: Definitely a bit repetitive
That voice is unbelievable, especially at the end after the key change. So hauntingly beautiful. No clear problems with any other part of the song. I might actually prefer the live version though
songs.name: Opera No.2
In My Own Time
songs.comments: The "la biblioteca donde está" line in the middle is incredibly odd.

songs.name: Cocoa Butter Kisses
songs.artists: Chance the Rapper, VIC MENSA, Twista

songs.name: Lovely Day
songs.comments: I like the few unique lyrics that exist but it honestly is far too repetitive

songs.name: It's Late - Remastered 2011
songs.album: News Of The World (Deluxe Remastered Version)

songs.name: The Nights
songs.comments: Could just be my headphones but the bass sounds so high and weak on this. Lyrics are pretty inspirational but the line "my father told me" isn't impactful enough for the place it's in

songs.name: Wake Me Up Before You Go-Go
songs.comments: Maybe a little repetitive. Vocals in some spots are definitely better but not throughout the whole song. Cohesion and background at the end are better but also not throughout.
I like the bridge a lot
songs.name: Back In The U.S.S.R. - Remastered 2009

songs.album: A Night At The Opera (Deluxe Remastered Version)
songs.name: '39 - Remastered 2011
When I Was Your Man
songs.comments: Bridge is outstanding and the final chorus concludes the song well. Some of the sections have vocals that are so unique and flawless I can't begin to imagine executing them

songs.name: Starman - 2012 Remaster
songs.album: The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and the Spiders from Mars (2012 Remaster)
songs.comments: Once again the chorus takes up so much of the song. I also just don't feel like the song has an overall direction

songs.name: Crazy Little Thing Called Love - Remastered 2011

songs.comments: I didn't deduct too much from the lyrics or flow despite the extreme repetition because he varies the way he sings them throughout.
songs.name: My Sweet Lord - Remastered 2014
Doo Wop (That Thing)
songs.comments: Reduced flow score because the song seems to meander a bit towards the end.

songs.name: Redbone
songs.comments: An incredibly recognizable instrumental. Gets a bit repetitive towards the second half.

songs.album: The Hunger Games: Songs From District 12 And Beyond
songs.name: Safe & Sound - from The Hunger Games Soundtrack
songs.comments: It's not quite as bad as it looks but having "ooh" be pretty much the only lyric in the second half isn't great.

songs.name: the words
songs.comments: Flow is barely passable. There is building in the background and some variation but still the chorus is repetitive.

songs.name: Saturday Night’s Alright (For Fighting) - Remastered 2014
songs.comments: As I'm noticing with many songs, the lyrics fall off in the second half. The slight variations in execution help a bit.

songs.name: Talking to the Moon
songs.comments: I feel that during the climax of the song the instrumental gets so overwhelming that it takes away the solemn vibe from the start of the song

songs.name: Take Me to Church
songs.comments: Slightly repetitive chorus
Also has an amazing music video. I think on paper the structure with 3 different verses from 2 different perspectives moving into "all the same" at the end is cool. The execution feels a bit flat.
songs.name: Tous les mêmes
Turn to Stone
songs.comments: I absolutely love this intro. Bridge is a little random. The repeats of the chorus generally have small differences which is nice.
Lyrics are a bit nonsensical but honestly it would probably work no matter what he was shouting
songs.name: Roadhouse Blues
Papaoutai
songs.comments: The music video for this song is incredible. The artist clearly has an emotional connection to what he's singing about. I love how it shifts from confusion to anger to sadness then climaxes and then reaches acceptance

songs.name: Thinking out Loud
songs.comments: Could maybe argue vocals can go up. The variation in the pre-chorus was nice and the final chorus is different so I didn't take away from flow. Bridge wasn't too inspiring though

songs.album: The Dark Side of the Moon
songs.name: Us and Them
La vie en rose - Single Version
songs.album: The Best Of The Decca Years Volume One: The Singer

songs.name: From The Start
songs.comments: Lyrics don't fit super well with the mood of the song in my opinion

songs.name: Can't Get It out of My Head

songs.album: Being Funny In A Foreign Language
songs.name: About You

songs.comments: Lyrics are a bit nonsensical. I don't know whether it's weird or interesting that they swap Molly and Desmond. There are occasionally these mouth sound effects that seem to overload the mic
songs.name: Ob-La-Di, Ob-La-Da - Remastered 2009
Can't You Hear Me Knocking - 2009 Mix
December, 1963 (Oh What a Night!)
songs.comments: I cannot believe that this is the drummer singing. His voice is absolutely gorgeous.
I like the lyrics but they also repeat a bit too much for me to raise the score.
songs.name: Pink Pony Club
songs.album: The Rise and Fall of a Midwest Princess

songs.name: Just Give Me a Reason (feat. Nate Ruess)
This Love
songs.album: Descendants Of The Sun Pt.3 (Original Television Soundtrack)

songs.comments: There's a better version on youtube. His pronunciation is much better there and his vocals blend much better. I'd give that one a 10 in vocals and not dock on cohesion. The song builds well though.
songs.name: S.O.S D'un Terrien en detresse - Live
Remember Summer Days
songs.comments: I like the song but it kind of lacks an overall direction. Some sections are directly repeated.

songs.name: 93 Million Miles
songs.album: Love Is a Four Letter Word (Deluxe Edition)

songs.name: Only the Good Die Young
songs.comments: I kind of feel like the lower instruments are too quiet on this one
I love the little drum fills. I'll keep saying it though, still too much chorus on all of these songs with no variation. It was a pretty good transition out of the chorus into the ending though
songs.name: Still into You

songs.comments: Unreal vocals. I think the key change stuff at the end is interesting but it gets a little repetitive and the fade out ending feels lazy
songs.name: Love On Top

songs.album: Soul Treasures of Amy, Mala, & Bell Records (1963-1969)
songs.name: There's Always Something There to Remind Me
songs.comments: The song was going somewhere during the line "if you should miss" and then it just killed all motion to go back to the chorus and meander to the end.
The repeating chorus makes up far too much of the song in my opinion
songs.name: Someone Like You
Marry You
songs.comments: Chorus repeats too many times in my opinion

songs.name: I Guess That's Why They Call It The Blues
Come A Little Bit Closer
songs.album: Come A Little Bit Closer: The Best Of Jay & The Americans

songs.name: Mayonaka no Door / Stay With Me

songs.comments: I really like his flow (lyrical flow and overall song flow are different). The background is actually pretty interesting too, some pretty unique chords there
songs.name: X-Wing - Cold Blooded Soul Version
songs.album: Melt My Eyez See Your Future (The Extended Edition)

songs.name: Raspberry Beret
songs.album: Around The World In A Day

songs.name: Have You Ever Seen The Rain

songs.comments: The lyrics from the first half of the song don't align with the lyrics from the second half
songs.name: Hey Ya!
(I Can't Get No) Satisfaction - Mono
songs.comments: Theme is pretty repetitive
To be fully honest I personally don't love this song that much but I feel when I assess each category it performs exceedingly well.
songs.name: My Way - 2008 Remastered

songs.comments: Although the repeated sections are executed different, there are essentially no unique lyrics outside of them
songs.name: Sway

songs.comments: Don't really like the bridge or the repetition towards the end
songs.name: Royals
September
songs.album: The Best Of Earth, Wind & Fire Vol. 1
songs.comments: Could debate that the cohesion should be increased or that the flow and lyrics should be decreased. I decided not to overcomplicate for now.

songs.name: Everybody Talks
songs.comments: Very little variation in the lyrics
I think the added vocal accents save the flow of this song in the last chorus. I really wish more songs did this
songs.name: Sugar

songs.comments: All the parts fit together well with the theme of the song. Some of the sections feel a bit odd or exhausting though because it's a very a long song so I reduced the flow.
songs.name: Kashmir - 1990 Remaster
The Power Of Love
songs.album: Greatest Hits: Huey Lewis And The News

songs.name: Olimpico
songs.comments: No 10 on vocals because he (understandably) does not sound completely natural in every register. Although chorus dominates lyrics, it's sung differently every time
The background is unbelievably repetitive which also makes the song a bit repetitive despite the changing lyrics
songs.name: It Was A Good Day
Just the Two of Us (feat. Bill Withers) - Edit
songs.album: Lean on Me: The Best of Bill Withers

songs.name: My City Was Gone
songs.comments: I was probably a little generous with the background but I just absolutely adore that bass line
I'm not a big fan of how it dips into the lower energy section, it feels a sudden and jarring
songs.name: Merry Christmas
songs.album: アイドル・ミラクルバイブルシリーズ　伊藤智恵理

songs.name: Blue - Bonus Track

songs.name: 浪子心聲 - 電影《半斤八両》歌曲
Livin' On A Prayer
songs.comments: I really wish it carried the story in the lyrics into the ending

songs.name: Shine a Little Love
songs.comments: Vocals don't blend very well and the lyrics are kind of all over the place even when they aren't just repeating the main line.
I think just repeating the chorus again is a very boring way to end the song and overall the song isn't very interesting after the second verse
songs.name: Team
I Saw Her Standing There - Remastered 2009

songs.album: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse (Soundtrack From & Inspired by the Motion Picture)
songs.name: Sunflower - Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
songs.comments: There are a lot of things I like and don't like about this song. The lyrics in the first verse feel a bit conflicting with some being poignant and others a bit crass. The vocals are nice aside from the autotune
The second verse is really good. The added vocals in the final chorus and the outro help the flow a lot.
songs.name: Who Says

songs.comments: The second half of the song has some strange garbled background vocals
songs.name: All of Me
Until the Night
songs.comments: 5 different verses, a bridge and only 3 choruses in a nearly 7 minute song is a great structure. There are so many different parts in the background
I don't want to be harsh because it's probably a stylistic choice but she sings very flat at some points and it sounds odd to me.
songs.name: Liquid Smooth

songs.comments: I really like the section starting with "so many faces in and out of my life"
songs.name: Say Goodbye to Hollywood
The Sound of Silence
songs.comments: The song builds well
I like how the song is clearly moving towards the climax and then transitions into an outro afterwards
songs.name: Steppin' Out

songs.comments: "How am I gonna be an optimist about this" is a pretty awkward line. Bridge is pretty good. The chorus is completely identical every time it plays.
songs.name: Pompeii

songs.comments: The drums are great. Lyrics get a little repetitive but the song is noticeably building and the chorus section in the outro is different. Could raise cohesion, I feel vocals don't meld perfectly
songs.name: This December

songs.comments: I don't dislike the medley but the switch does slightly impact flow
songs.name: Scenes from an Italian Restaurant

songs.comments: The song blends beautifully at the chorus
songs.album: The Land Is Inhospitable and So Are We
songs.name: My Love Mine All Mine
Gimme! Gimme! Gimme! (A Man After Midnight)
Turn the Lights Off
songs.comments: Pretty solid structure with 4 different verses, a full bridge and a key change.
Seems to be consistent theme with Van Halen that there isn't a lot of thought put towards the lyrics in the second half of the song. I get that it's not the focus but still
songs.name: Runnin' with the Devil

songs.comments: The structure is really interesting with the interlude and the refrain. Overall just melds together very well, rapping is well executed, lyrics are a bit random but the words "sound" good.
songs.name: We Major
Dreams
songs.album: Everybody Else Is Doing It, So Why Can't We?
A Night At The Opera (Deluxe Remastered Version)
songs.name: You're My Best Friend - Remastered 2011

songs.comments: Not a huge fan of the repeat on the chorus but it builds well at the end

Diamonds From Sierra Leone - Bonus Track

songs.album: Songs From The Big Chair (Super Deluxe Edition)
songs.name: Everybody Wants To Rule The World
Vienna
songs.comments: Tons of unique lyrics in this one that have a lot of intention. It builds and dies down which gives it good flow.

songs.name: Y.M.C.A.
songs.comments: I was bit on the fence about vocals lyrics and flow here. Vocals are solid but nothing really pops out. Some words and phrases are repetitive and the song stays at the same energy level throughout.

songs.name: My Life
songs.comments: I don't like how the post-chorus repeats. I like the lyrics in the two unique verses but the exact repeat of the post-chorus lyrics is why I'm not raising the lyric score further.
The story drastically improves the flow of the song
songs.name: El Paso

songs.album: Ladies And Gentlemen... The Best Of George Michael
songs.name: Careless Whisper

songs.comments: The scraping noise on the right side is a bit odd towards the beginning. I love how faithfully the instrumental follows the vocals through the different sections. Chorus is a bit repetitive.
songs.name: Shut Up and Dance

songs.comments: The bridge was what initially sold me on this song, it's very nice. The variation on the chorus at the end is also very well done. I might even consider upping the vocal score on this one
songs.name: Style
Leave The Door Open
songs.comments: I feel a little bit like the song doesn't go anywhere but the instrumental is very interesting

songs.album: If You Can Believe Your Eyes & Ears
songs.name: California Dreamin' - Single Version
Somebody That I Used To Know
songs.comments: I feel like the second half could have blended much better but the background vocals frequently started and stopped instead of being sustained.

songs.album: The Rise and Fall of a Midwest Princess
songs.name: HOT TO GO!
Till I Collapse
songs.comments: This was a really difficult song to grade. Still don't really know how to approach it
This song is hard to grade because it's very solid but also very simple. That makes it difficult to raise any score even though lowering one would be out of the question.
songs.album: You Don't Mess Around With Jim

Belize (feat. MF DOOM)

I feel like this should be higher but it’s kind of basic. Maybe I’m wrong.
songs.name: She's Always a Woman
Walk This Way
songs.comments: Lyrics are definitely a little weird in isolation but they fit well with the vibe of the song. Lyrics are a little repetitive and the song doesn't really go anywhere

songs.name: テレフォン・ナンバー
Love Grows (Where My Rosemary Goes)
songs.album: Love Grows (Where My Rosemary Goes) & Other Gems

songs.name: Long Cool Woman (In a Black Dress) - 1999 Remaster

Serendipity

songs.comments: The background vocals add a lot
I don't really like the percussion in the background (clapping I think?). It feels a bit out of place to me and makes the song feel too casual
songs.name: I Wish You Love
Happy - From "Despicable Me 2"
songs.comments: The song gets very repetitive at the end. I honestly feel like it would've been better if it ended at the 2:43 mark instead of going for another repetition.
I don't like the kind of techno style that comes in at the second half. Her voice also sounds like it's being garbled by pitch correction.
songs.name: Dernière danse
Love Me Two Times
songs.comments: Lyrics feel a bit nonsensical and repetitive

songs.name: Faded
songs.comments: Definitely not my preferred music genre but I gotta admit it's pretty hype

songs.comments: The bridge is a bit odd but it leads well into the next section.

songs.comments: A lot of chorus but it changes at the end and I love the bridge.
songs.name: This Love
seasons

A very good example of building throughout the song and changing the delivery on the same lyric

songs.comments: Flow score reduced because I feel like the bridge doesn't fit perfectly and because the song keeps the same format until the last verse

songs.name: Dude (Looks Like A Lady)

The dreaded 2 verse 3 chorus flow. And no real bridge. At least they introduce some more instruments later I guess.

songs.comments: I like the first section a lot more than the rest

songs.name: The Way You Make Me Feel - 2012 Remaster

songs.comments: Although he does change the way he sings the sections when he repeats, the song still has a very odd structure where it essentially repeats three times with no bridge

Ain't It a Drag

Chorus repeats too many times in my opinion

logs.songName: Mayonaka no Door / Stay With Me

logs.comments: I'm not a big fan of how it dips into the lower energy section, it feels a sudden and jarring
I was probably a little generous with the background but I just absolutely adore that bass line

logs.songName: My Heart Is Buried in Venice

logs.comments: I don't like how the post-chorus repeats. I like the lyrics in the two unique verses but the exact repeat of the post-chorus lyrics is why I'm not raising the lyric score further.
The song blends beautifully at the chorus
I didn't deduct too much from the lyrics or flow despite the extreme repetition because he varies the way he sings them throughout.

logs.songName: Fly Me To The Moon - 2008 Remastered

logs.comments: To be fully honest I personally don't love this song that much but I feel when I assess each category it performs exceedingly well. I likely even underrated it due to my bias.
To be fully honest I personally don't love this song that much but I feel when I assess each category it performs exceedingly well.
I kind of feel like the lower instruments are too quiet on this one
I kind of feel like the lower instruments are too quiet on this one

The music video for this song is incredible. The artist clearly has an emotional connection to what he's singing about. I love how it shifts from confusion to anger to sadness then climaxes and then reaches acceptance
The song builds very well to the climax. All the parts work together to create the kind of dingy atmosphere.
All the parts work together to create the kind of dingy atmosphere. I do like how the song builds but I can't raise the flow because it repeats the same pattern so many times
I like the lyrics but they also repeat a bit too much for me to raise the score.
Definitely one of my favorite background instrumentals
I feel a little bit like the song doesn't go anywhere but the instrumental is very interesting
I really wish it carried the story in the lyrics into the ending
Solid mix of lyrics that I like and don't like so I'd say it evens out
Solid mix of lyrics that I like and don't like so I'd say it evens out. Vocals at the end are really good but they aren't that good throughout the whole song
I like the song but it kind of lacks an overall direction. Some sections are directly repeated.
Lyrics are a bit nonsensical but honestly it would probably work no matter what he was shouting
Don't really like the bridge or the repetition towards the end
Seems to be consistent theme with Van Halen that there isn't a lot of thought put towards the lyrics in the second half of the song. I get that it's not the focus but still
Could just be my headphones but the bass sounds so high and weak on this. Lyrics are pretty inspirational but the line "my father told me" isn't impactful enough for the place it's in
Lyrics are a bit nonsensical. I don't know whether it's weird or interesting that they swap Molly and Desmond. There are occasionally these mouth sound effects that seem to overload the mic
Lyrics and delivery in the intro are incredible. Aside from that it gets a bit repetitive. The synth around the middle is very disorienting with headphones on.
"How am I gonna be an optimist about this" is a pretty awkward line. Bridge is pretty good. The chorus is completely identical every time it plays.
An incredibly recognizable instrumental. Gets a bit repetitive towards the second half.

logs.songName: Safe & Sound - from The Hunger Games Soundtrack
logs.comments: It's not quite as bad as it looks but having "ooh" be pretty much the only lyric in the second half isn't great.
As I'm noticing with many songs, the lyrics fall off in the second half. The slight variations in execution help a bit.
I really like the section starting with "so many faces in and out of my life"
I really like the section starting with "so many faces in and out of my life"

logs.artists: A Great Big World, Christina Aguilera

logs.comments: Not a fan of the second verse. Pronunciation isn't bad but vocals would be improved if it was better.
I don't dislike the medley but the switch does slightly impact flow
A very good example of building throughout the song and changing the delivery on the same lyric
logs.artists: A Great Big World, Christina Aguilera

logs.comments: Could debate that the cohesion should be increased or that the flow and lyrics should be decreased. I decided not to overcomplicate for now.
Lyrics are overall pretty solid on this one but I will mention that I don’t like when lyrics include a specific number of unknown significance like “twenty-three”
I feel like this should be higher but it’s kind of basic. Maybe I’m wrong.
Vocals don't blend very well and the lyrics are kind of all over the place even when they aren't just repeating the main line.
Lyrics aren't bad but again I wish there was more variance in the second half.
Lyrics aren't bad but again I wish there was more variance in the second half.
The scraping noise on the right side is a bit odd towards the beginning. I love how faithfully the instrumental follows the vocals through the different sections. Chorus is a bit repetitive.

logs.songName: Somebody That I Used To Know
logs.comments: I feel like the second half could have blended much better but the background vocals frequently started and stopped instead of being sustained.
I don't like the heavily autotuned section but aside from that he's clearly a good singer.
I don't like the heavily autotuned section but aside from that he's clearly a good singer.
The repeating chorus makes up far too much of the song in my opinion
The vocals, particularly during the bridge, are excellent. The chorus dominates too much of the song but I didn't lower the score for now.
The song builds well
The vocal score feels slightly too high and the background score slightly too low so I figure it kind of balances out
This is a hard one. I think vocals, background and cohesion all could debatably be increased
This was a pretty confusing one to grade. The instrumental and vocals have a lot of raw emotion. The pre chorus and chorus still make up too much of the song.
Once again the chorus takes up so much of the song. I also just don't feel like the song has an overall direction
No 10 on vocals because he (understandably) does not sound completely natural in every register. Although chorus dominates lyrics, it's sung differently every time
That voice is unbelievable, especially at the end after the key change. So hauntingly beautiful. No clear problems with any other part of the song
That voice is unbelievable, especially at the end after the key change. So hauntingly beautiful. No clear problems with any other part of the song. I might actually prefer the live version though
There's a better version on youtube. His pronunciation is much better there and his vocals blend much better. I'd give that one a 10 in vocals and not dock on cohesion. The song builds well though.
logs.songName: S.O.S D'un Terrien en detresse - Live

logs.comments: A good example of how even a small change to a line in the chorus can improve the lyrics. Even so I could see the lyrics and flow being lower
I like how the song is clearly moving towards the climax and then transitions into an outro afterwards
I love the little drum fills. I'll keep saying it though, still too much chorus on all of these songs with no variation. It was a pretty good transition out of the chorus into the ending though
The bridge was what initially sold me on this song, it's very nice. The variation on the chorus at the end is also very well done. I might even consider upping the vocal score on this one
I think the added vocal accents save the flow of this song in the last chorus. I really wish more songs did this
Bridge is a little odd but it recovers well into the next section

There are a lot of things I like and don't like about this song. The lyrics in the first verse feel a bit conflicting with some being poignant and others a bit crass. The vocals are nice aside from the autotune
Although the repeated sections are executed different, there are essentially no unique lyrics outside of them
The vocals are very impressive. I took a bit off the flow because in the second half the song kind of devolves into chaos. Good sounding chaos, but still nonsensical
Slightly repetitive chorus
Although the repeated sections are executed different, there are essentially no unique lyrics outside of them
I feel that during the climax of the song the instrumental gets so overwhelming that it takes away the solemn vibe from the start of the song
I think just repeating the chorus again is a very boring way to end the song and overall the song isn't very interesting after the second verse
The song cuts off very suddenly. I definitely think you could justify raising the background score
I like the theme of the lyrics being advice. There are a lot of unique lines.

logs.songName: There's Always Something There to Remind Me
logs.comments: The song was going somewhere during the line "if you should miss" and then it just killed all motion to go back to the chorus and meander to the end.
Could maybe argue vocals can go up. The variation in the pre-chorus was nice and the final chorus is different so I didn't take away from flow. Bridge wasn't too inspiring though
The drums are great. Lyrics get a little repetitive but the song is noticeably building and the chorus section in the outro is different. Could raise cohesion, I feel vocals don't meld perfectly
A lot of chorus but it changes at the end and I love the bridge.
The dreaded 2 verse 3 chorus flow. And no real bridge. At least they introduce some more instruments later I guess.
This song is hard to grade because it's very solid but also very simple. That makes it difficult to raise any score even though lowering one would be out of the question.
I find his voice a bit grating but you could argue it also has a lot of emotion.
I find his voice a bit grating but you could argue it also has a lot of emotion.
Pretty solid structure with 4 different verses, a full bridge and a key change.
I absolutely love this intro. Bridge is a little random. The repeats of the chorus generally have small differences which is nice.
5 different verses, a bridge and only 3 choruses in a nearly 7 minute song is a great structure.
I find his voice a bit grating but you could argue it also has a lot of emotion.
The background vocals add a lot
5 different verses, a bridge and only 3 choruses in a nearly 7 minute song is a great structure. There are so many different parts in the background
Tons of unique lyrics in this one that have a lot of intention. It builds and dies down which gives it good flow.
Tons of unique lyrics in this one that have a lot of intention. It builds and dies down which gives it good flow.
Maybe a little repetitive. Vocals in some spots are definitely better but not throughout the whole song. Cohesion and background at the end are better but also not throughout.

logs.songName: Wake Me Up Before You Go-Go
logs.comments: Maybe a little repetitive. Vocals in some spots are definitely better but not throughout the whole song. Cohesion and background at the end are better but also not throughout.
Lyrics are definitely a little weird in isolation but they fit well with the vibe of the song. Lyrics are a little repetitive and the song doesn't really go anywhere

logs.songName: The Way You Make Me Feel - 2012 Remaster
logs.comments: The song is very long and meanders a bit. The sections that are clearly designed for dance breaks in a live performance don't help the flow either.
Nice outro. Kind of saves the flow. Could argue that vocal score should be higher.
Some of the sections are a little odd. You could argue that the vocal score should be higher.
The structure is really interesting with the interlude and the refrain. Overall just melds together very well, rapping is well executed, lyrics are a bit random but the words "sound" good.
The vocals are nice but almost a little too breathy even if that's the intended style.
logs.songName: What Was I Made For? [From The Motion Picture "Barbie"]

logs.comments: Bridge is outstanding and the final chorus concludes the song well. Some of the sections have vocals that are so unique and flawless I can't begin to imagine executing them
The second verse is really good. The added vocals in the final chorus and the outro help the flow a lot.
The vocals are excellent but they do stick out a little bit. I actually love the background vocals in the outro.
Flow is barely passable. There is building in the background and some variation but still the chorus is repetitive.
Background vocals do a lot for this song
I really like his flow (lyrical flow and overall song flow are different). The background is actually pretty interesting too, some pretty unique chords there
This was a really difficult song to grade. Still don't really know how to approach it
Also has an amazing music video. I think on paper the structure with 3 different verses from 2 different perspectives moving into "all the same" at the end is cool. The execution feels a bit flat.
I was bit on the fence about vocals lyrics and flow here. Vocals are solid but nothing really pops out. Some words and phrases are repetitive and the song stays at the same energy level throughout.
